title: Correcting the Optimized Binary Search Function

statement.markdown: Given an array of integers and a target integer, implement an optimized binary search function. The function should return the index of the target integer if it exists; otherwise, it should return -1. The function should have a time complexity of O(log(n)) and should handle duplicate elements correctly. However, the given implementation contains a subtle logical bug that prevents it from working correctly for all inputs.
statement.inputFormat: The function takes three inputs: an array of integers `arr`, a target integer `target`, and an optional boolean flag `find_first_occurrence`.
statement.outputFormat: The function returns the index of the target integer if it exists; otherwise, it returns -1.
statement.notes: The function should have a time complexity of O(log(n)) and should handle duplicate elements correctly.

constraints.description: The number of elements in the input array
The target integer to search for

examples.explanation: The function should return the index of the first occurrence of the target integer 9, which is 3.
The function should return the index of the first occurrence of the target integer 2, which is 1.

hints.text: The given implementation contains a subtle logical bug that prevents it from working correctly for all inputs.
The time complexity of the function should be O(log(n)) to ensure efficient search.

editorial.title: Correcting the Optimized Binary Search Function
editorial.intuition: To correct the implementation, we need to handle the edge cases correctly and ensure that the function returns the index of the first occurrence of the target integer.
editorial.bruteForce: The brute force approach would involve iterating through the array in linear time, which would result in a time complexity of O(n).
editorial.betterSolution: We can use a modified binary search algorithm to search for the target integer in O(log(n)) time.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution involves using a recursive binary search function to find the target integer.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness involves showing that the function returns the correct index of the target integer for all possible inputs.
editorial.dryRun: A dry run of the function would involve testing it with different inputs to ensure that it works correctly.
commonMistakes: Not ensuring a time complexity of O(log(n))

functionSignature.cpp: int binarySearch(vector<int>& arr, int target, bool find_first_occurrence);
functionSignature.java: int binarySearch(int[] arr, int target, boolean find_first_occurrence);
